claim: In August 2016, the Israeli Ministry of Diaspora Affairs (then led by Naftali Bennett, later Prime Minister of Israel in 2021-22) launched Mosaic United — a $66 million joint initiative with three partner organizations operating on U.S. college campuses: Hillel International, Olami Worldwide, and Chabad on Campus. The Israeli government's announced contribution was approximately $33 million, with the partner organizations matching the figure to roughly $66 million in total program funding. Hillel International's share was approximately $22 million over five years. The initiative's stated purpose was “strengthening Jewish identity” on U.S. campuses and countering the Boycott, Divestment, and Sanctions movement. None of the three U.S.-side recipient organizations registered as foreign agents under the Foreign Agents Registration Act (FARA, 22 U.S.C. § 611 et seq.), which generally requires U.S. entities receiving funds or direction from foreign governments to disclose those relationships to the Department of Justice. The student-organized Open Hillel campaign formally called on Hillel to reject the Israeli-government funding in November 2016. Hillel continued the partnership.

sources.label: Jewish Telegraphic Agency — “Open Hillel calls on Hillel to reject funding by Naftali Bennett project” (November 3, 2016).
The Forward — “Open Hillel Calls on Hillel To Reject of $22M in Israel Government Funding” (November 4, 2016).
Influence Watch — Hillel International organizational profile, with funding and partnership history.
U.S. Department of Justice — Foreign Agents Registration Act (FARA) registration search portal.

notes: Connects to existing y2018-nation-state-law records (the Israel-apartheid framework, the anti-BDS state laws). The FARA-registration gap is a documented matter of dispute: the Israeli Ministry of Diaspora Affairs is a foreign government, the funding is a contractual relationship, but Hillel maintains it does not act under foreign 'direction or control' within FARA's meaning. The DOJ has not opened a FARA enforcement action against Hillel through mid-2026.
